facts.text: Madison Historical Society’s 2026 exhibit celebrates 200 years of Madison, spotlighting Captain Frederick Lee and the town’s Revolutionary War role.
The Madison Historical Society sits in Lee’s Academy on 14 Meetinghouse Lane, open Wednesday through Friday from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m.
Madison Center lies at the crossroads of U.S. Route 1 and Connecticut Route 79, anchoring the town’s historic core.
The census-designated place extends south to Long Island Sound, north to Interstate 95, east to Fence Creek, and west to Long Shore Land and Stony Lane.
The Society’s mission is to promote public appreciation of Madison’s historic character and encourage stewardship of its early buildings.
Join the community for the Welcome Reception on May 1 from 4 p.m. to 7 p.m., a highlight of Madison’s cultural calendar.
